title: Cooperation between cGAS and RIG-I sensing pathways enables improved innate recognition of HIV-1 by myeloid dendritic cells in elite controllers
author: Martin Gayo, Enrique
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: In this study, we identified the cooperation of the cGAS and RIG-I pathways in enhancing innate sensing of HIV-1 in dendritic cells from Elite Controllers. This cooperation is mediated by the  activity of RNA polymerase III which leads to viral RNA synthesis from viral DNA allowing the sensing simultaneous sensing of the RIG-I pathway and the activation of specific T cell responses.
steps_raw: In this study, we identified the cooperation of the cGAS and RIG-I pathways in enhancing innate sensing of HIV-1 in dendritic cells from Elite Controllers. This cooperation is mediated by the  activity of RNA polymerase III which leads to viral RNA synthesis from viral DNA allowing the sensing simultaneous sensing of the RIG-I pathway and the activation of specific T cell responses.
